title: Length & Searching 🔎
description: Find exactly what you are looking for inside massive strings.

slides.title: Measuring Length 📏
Finding Text 🔎
slides.content:
## indexOf()

How do we know if a paragraph contains a specific word?

We use the **`.indexOf()`** method. You pass it a search string, and it will return the **exact starting index number** where it found that text!

```javascript
let phrase = "I love coding in JavaScript";

console.log( phrase.indexOf("coding") ); // Outputs: 7
```

The word "coding" starts at the 7th indexed character of the string!

slides.title: Missing Text? 👻
slides.content: What happens if .indexOf() searches for a word but cannot find it anywhere?

quizData.question: What number does `.indexOf()` return if it CANNOT find the text you are searching for?
quizData.explanation: It returns -1. Why? Because 0 is a valid index! If your search word is at the very beginning of the string, it will return 0.

slides.title: The Modern Solution ✨

quizData.question: Is `.includes()` case-sensitive? Would `"Apple".includes("apple")` return true or false?

options.text: False. It perfectly matches the casing.

quizData.explanation: JavaScript is fiercely case-sensitive. Big 'A' and little 'a' are completely different characters to the computer. A common trick is to run .toLowerCase() on the string before you search it!
